basics.name: Subhajit Chowdhury
basics.title: Data Engineer
basics.summary: Cloud-focused Data Engineer with over 3.9 years of experience in designing, building and maintaining production-grade ETL/ELT data pipelines on AWS, spanning batch processing, near real-time event-driven ingestion, and scalable data lake architectures on Amazon S3 integrated with cloud data warehouses. Hands-on experience with AWS Glue (PySpark), EMR, Redshift, Athena, Step Functions, Lambda, DynamoDB, CloudWatch and Snowflake. Strong understanding of medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold), metadata-driven ingestion, and incremental data processing (CDC/SCD), along with SQL performance optimization. Experienced in managing the end-to-end data pipeline lifecycle, including ingestion, transformation, data quality, monitoring, and production support, with exposure to CI/CD-based deployments using Git and Jenkins. Open to cloud data engineering roles - remote or hybrid – where I can work on high-scale data systems and keep building depth across the modern data stack.

bullets: Built and maintained 150+ production-grade AWS ETL/ELT pipelines (S3, Glue, EMR, Redshift, Athena) on a medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold), handling data at MB, GB, and TB scale daily — delivering analytics-ready datasets to downstream BI and reporting consumers.

Standardized data lake storage on Delta Lake on S3 as the production standard, alongside Parquet and other formats — enabling ACID transactions, schema enforcement, and efficient upserts across the data lake.
Implemented a metadata-driven ingestion framework using AWS Glue Crawlers for automated schema discovery across Parquet, JSON, and CSV formats — reducing downstream data latency by ~30%.
Orchestrated batch ETL workflows using AWS Step Functions with retry logic and failure handling — reducing manual intervention and improving execution reliability in production.
Developed SQL and PySpark transformations with incremental load logic (insert/update/delete) aligned with star schema and dimensional modeling, keeping Redshift tables accurate and up to date.
Set up CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and Git for version-controlled, repeatable deployments across dev, test, and production environments.
Optimized SQL query performance by ~20–25% through partition pruning, predicate pushdown, and efficient joins in Redshift and Athena — reducing unnecessary data scans.
Monitored production pipelines using CloudWatch logs, alerts, and dashboards — detecting and resolving issues before downstream impact.
Reduced compute and storage costs by optimizing S3 storage patterns, query structures, and scan footprints across Glue and Athena workloads.
Collaborated with analytics, QA, and BI teams across full Agile delivery cycles — sprint planning, backlog refinement, and production support.
Worked on building a cloud data lakehouse on AWS, migrating and consolidating data from on-premises sources into a scalable, multi-layer architecture on S3.

Data ingested via AWS DMS into S3, structured across Bronze, Silver, and Gold layers following medallion architecture, with Delta Lake as the storage standard — enabling ACID transactions, schema enforcement, and reliable upserts.
Built transformation and processing jobs using AWS Glue (PySpark) and Lambda, orchestrated via AWS Step Functions with retry logic and failure handling.

Delivered data to multiple downstream consumers based on business requirements — Athena for SQL-based querying, Amazon QuickSight for reporting and dashboards, PostgreSQL for cost-efficient query results via S3, Redshift for analytical workloads, Snowflake for specific project requirements, and DynamoDB where needed.
Worked within an Agile team across sprint planning, Jira-based task management, and collaboration with QA, analytics, and BI stakeholders.

experience.role: Data Engineer | Assistant System Engineer - Trainee
bullets: Migrated ETL pipelines from Informatica PowerCenter to Informatica Intelligent Cloud Services (IICS), handling diverse input and output data types across source and target systems.
Developed ETL mappings end-to-end, built solution design documentation in Confluence, and used Oracle SQL for data extraction and validation.
Worked on migrating data from an on-premises Oracle database to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), handling data integration and ensuring data consistency across source and target systems.
Developed and tested ETL pipelines in IICS, performed data cleaning using Oracle SQL, and conducted unit testing, sanity testing, and system integration testing (SIT).
Managed code using GitHub within an Agile team on Jira throughout both projects.

projects.title: Serverless-Batch-Analytics-ETL-Pipeline-on-AWS
projects.description: Designed and implemented a fully serverless batch ETL pipeline on AWS to ingest, process, and structure analytical datasets for downstream reporting. Built a scalable data lake architecture with raw and curated layers (medallion-style). Enabled serverless, cost-efficient analytics by integrating the AWS Glue Data Catalog with Amazon Athena. Delivered end-to-end analytics by connecting Amazon Athena to Amazon QuickSight.

projects.title: Near-Real-Time-Serverless-ELT-Pipeline-on-AWS-with-Snowflake
projects.description: Built a near-real-time, event-driven ELT pipeline to ingest and process live API data with low latency. Solved challenges related to continuous ingestion and semi-structured JSON data handling. Implemented Snowflake Snowpipe for continuous, automated data ingestion from S3 into Snowflake tables. Applied ELT best practices by performing analytical transformations using Snowflake SQL, improving scalability and query performance.

items: AWS (S3, Glue, Lambda, Step Functions, Athena, Redshift, DynamoDB, CloudWatch, IAM, EventBridge)

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech), Computer Science and Engineering
education.institution: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology, West Bengal (MAKAUT)

extra: Strong focus on data reliability, scalability, and clean pipeline design
Comfortable working in Linux and Windows environments
Open to remote, hybrid, and international opportunities
